sets.interview_summary: Tony praises Michael as a legend from the Chicago improv scene who started stand-up after his ALS diagnosis. Ron White expresses deep respect for him.

sets.set_transcript: Another World Cup soccer journalist has died. But at this point are we sure he isn't just flopping? Biden and Russia have just completed a very festive prisoner swap right in time for the holiday season. Biden released an arms dealer nicknamed 'The Merchant of Death' who is responsible for deaths of thousands of American soldiers in exchange for WNBA player Brittney Griner. It's pretty obvious why Biden traded for Griner because she checks off three very important boxes in the current political climate: tall, check; likes cereal, check; enjoys music, check. Those aren't really the fucking reasons, you idiot. I mean holy shit, do I have to spell it out for you? I was watching the Price is Right earlier and I couldn't believe the prices for one of the showcases: a home gym, Kate Spade accessories, and a new Aphex Twin album! Okay, that's my time. Thank you.
sets.interview_summary: Tony discusses William's success story of getting sober from cocaine and alcohol, and how major comedians like Tom Segura are fighting over him for opening spots.

sets.set_transcript: Thanks everybody. Oh shit. How's it going everybody? Didn't think I'd be opening tonight. So, when I was nine years old, I saw something I'd never, never forget. Lived in the hood up north in Wisconsin. And these, these two black guys were, uh, arguing right in the middle of the street. Just, a little kid looking outside the window being nosey. And they, uh, they literally whipped out his dick mid-conversation in the middle of the argument and just started pissing right in the middle of the street, right in the middle of the argument. And I, I'd never forget it. And looking back, I, I wish I had the confidence to do that because that's a fucking power move right there. Like just being able to do that. And the other guy didn't know what to do. So I mean like it's a definitely a conversation ender. Like that's something I'd never forget. That's my time.
sets.interview_summary: Matt's first time doing stand-up. Tony and Ron White criticize his microphone skills and lack of a structured joke plan. He works security at a chicken rendering plant.

sets.interview_summary: Ashley is a server at Pelons and an Austin local. Tony is very impressed by his performance and presence, giving him a handmade leather joke book.

sets.interview_summary: Tony scolds Zach for bailing on his written joke to attempt crowd work that didn't land. Zach moved from Buffalo to Austin five weeks prior.
